title: feat(quadlet): add SocketActivationPort — systemd socket activation for containers and pods
body:
## Motivation

Containers in Podman lack native socket activation through Quadlet.
Currently, achieving on-demand container startup requires manually writing
three systemd units (.socket, proxy, .service) — the "OnlyOffice" pattern
documented in tutorials. This means dozens of boilerplate lines per port.

This PR adds the `SocketActivationPort` key to Quadlet — one line replaces
three manual units. Multiple ports are supported (like PublishPort), it works
in both rootless and root modes, and requires no container modification.

`SocketActivationPort` is fully compatible with and reuses `PublishPort`:
- Same syntax (`hostPort:containerPort`)
- Same parser (`specgenutil.CreatePortBindings`)
- Coexists in the same unit with collision detection
- PublishPort range conflict detection prevents runtime EADDRINUSE

## Design

systemd-socket-proxyd was chosen over direct fd-passing because:
- Works with ANY container image (no LISTEN_FDS support required)
- Supports port remapping (hostPort ≠ containerPort)
- Isolates the container from external networks (loopback-only internal port)

The proxy receives 14 hardening directives (SystemCallFilter, RestrictSUIDSGID,
etc.), a readiness probe with fallback chain (socat → python3 → bash), and
auto-allocates internal ports with floor=1024 for rootless safety.

## Limitations (v1 / known tradeoffs)

- TCP only (systemd-socket-proxyd limitation)
- No idle-shutdown — containers start on demand but are not auto-stopped
- Readiness probe depends on socat/python3/bash (Go wait-helper planned)

## Testing Done

- 30+ unit tests: all parse errors, multi-port, port collisions, hardening
- go build ./... ✅
- go vet ✅
- gofumpt ✅
- xref-quadlet-docs ✅
- Manual dry-run verification with various .container/.pod configurations

## Future Work (follow-up PRs)

- Go wait-port helper to replace socat/python3/bash readiness probe
- [Kube] group support
- Direct fd-passing when hostPort == containerPort (v2 hybrid)
